items.title: Always declare `[build-system]` and `requires-python`
items.avoid: Omitting `[build-system]` (pip falls back to legacy setuptools — surprises) or `requires-python` (pip installs on incompatible interpreters and your code breaks at runtime).
items.prefer: Two small blocks at the top of every `pyproject.toml`.
items.why: Tiny declarations that prevent the two most common installation surprises.

items.title: Declare abstract deps in `pyproject.toml`; pin in lockfiles
items.avoid: Strict pins (`httpx==0.27.2`) in a library's `[project] dependencies` — they conflict with other libs.
items.prefer: Compatible ranges (`httpx>=0.27,<1`) in `pyproject.toml`; exact pins in a lockfile (`requirements.lock`, `uv.lock`).
items.why: Libraries should give *ranges* so they compose; deployment envs need *pins* for reproducibility. Different artefacts, different jobs.

items.title: Use `[project.optional-dependencies]` for dev tooling
items.avoid: Forcing every install to pull in pytest, mypy, ruff, docs tools.
items.prefer: Group dev deps under an extra: `[project.optional-dependencies] dev = [...]`; contributors run `pip install -e '.[dev]'`.
items.why: Production installs stay lean; contributor setup is one command. Same project, two install profiles.

items.title: Co-locate tool config in `[tool.*]`
items.avoid: Scattering config across `setup.cfg`, `.flake8`, `mypy.ini`, `pytest.ini`, `tox.ini` — five dotfiles for five tools.
items.prefer: All tool config in `pyproject.toml` under `[tool.<name>]` tables.
items.why: One file beats five for navigability and onboarding; less drift, fewer places to look.

items.title: Validate by building and inspecting before publishing
items.avoid: Running `twine upload` directly from CI without inspecting the artefact.
items.prefer: Build locally, list contents, run `twine check`, smoke-install into a fresh venv, *then* upload.
items.why: Confirms files included, metadata correct, no extra junk — yanking a release is painful.
items.example:
```bash
python -m build
unzip -l dist/mypkg-*.whl
twine check dist/*
pip install dist/*.whl --target /tmp/smoke
```
